---
title: "News"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.container}
# News & Updates

This page highlights notable changes, milestones, and documentation updates for DPmixGPD. It is intentionally concise. Detailed technical changes live in commit history and pkgdown documentation.

[View GitHub Releases](https://github.com/arnabaich96/DPmixGPD/releases){.btn .btn-primary}
[Repository Activity](https://github.com/arnabaich96/DPmixGPD/commits){.btn .btn-outline-light}
:::
:::

::: {.section}
::: {.container}

## Recent highlights

::: {.grid-3}

::: {.cardx}
### Website restructuring
A new Quarto-based landing site now serves as the curated front door for DPmixGPD. It provides:
- conceptual navigation,
- decision guides,
- workflow orientation,
while the full API reference remains in the vanilla pkgdown site.

This separation improves readability without sacrificing completeness.
:::

::: {.cardx}
### Documentation consolidation
Vignettes, reference pages, and workflow explanations have been reorganized for clarity:
- Quarto pages focus on *what to do and why*,
- pkgdown pages focus on *functions and arguments*.
This reduces duplication and broken links across documentation layers.
:::

::: {.cardx}
### Infrastructure stabilization
The documentation build pipeline has been stabilized to:
- render Quarto pages deterministically,
- isolate pkgdown output,
- avoid unnecessary rebuilds for small content changes.
This improves local development speed and GitHub Pages reliability.
:::

:::

:::
:::

::: {.section}
::: {.container}

## Ongoing work

::: {.grid-3}

::: {.cardx}
### Expanded decision guides
Clearer guidance for:
- kernel selection,
- tail modeling decisions,
- unconditional vs conditional vs causal workflows.
:::

::: {.cardx}
### Coverage and diagnostics
Work is ongoing to improve:
- test coverage for core workflows,
- clarity around diagnostic outputs,
- visibility of coverage signals on the website.
:::

::: {.cardx}
### Causal and survival extensions
Continued refinement of:
- treatment effect summaries,
- survival-tail workflows,
- consistency across predictive interfaces.
:::

:::

:::
:::

::: {.section}
::: {.container}

## Versioning policy

DPmixGPD follows a pragmatic development model:
- breaking changes are avoided unless necessary,
- documentation is updated alongside behavioral changes,
- version numbers reflect user-facing impact rather than internal refactors.

For reproducibility, always record the package version and build date used in analysis.

## Where to follow development

- Feature ideas and design discussions:  
  https://github.com/arnabaich96/DPmixGPD/discussions
- Bug reports and actionable issues:  
  https://github.com/arnabaich96/DPmixGPD/issues
- Code changes and history:  
  https://github.com/arnabaich96/DPmixGPD/commits

This page will be updated periodically to reflect major changes or milestones.

:::
:::
